channel.title: Comments for page "Tying the Gordian Knot"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Tying the Gordian Knot"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kind of felt the same way—there was a lot happening here, and I wasn't super familiar with the main characters. That said, I still really enjoyed the read, since there were snippets like:

But upon further review, he came to realize just how much engineering went into this cell. SRA mounted underneath the floor. Full camera coverage. Concrete inlaid with anti-pretty-much-everything sigils. Fentanyl gas dispenser in the ceiling. VX gas dispenser next to that.

I headcanon the Foundation as super competent and seeing that nod to the details involved in containment engineering sold the setting for me.

And plus the occasional gem in the dialogue, like:

"I uh, don't have anything. Just the clothes on my back."

"Then the wedding cannot continue!"

That sort of atypical conversation played entirely straight made me grin. I'll have to add Anoati to my "read this next" list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually sympathized with Anoati during all this. The things a god has to do to get decent help…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is hilariously fucked up. I haven't kept up with site fiction in forever so I only barely understood what was going on, but I enjoyed it despite that.

item.title: Technically a Romantic Comedy
item.content:encoded:
My OCT tale is up! Given the tricky set of characters, it was hard to come up with something that made sense!

Huge thanks to psul, Attila the Pun, rounderhouse, and especially DarkStuff for critting this!

Make sure to read Attila the Pun's entry, Puppets and their Masters, and DrDromeus entry, whenever they get around to posting!
